evals.prompt: I need backend tests for a Fastify endpoint that calls a partner REST API and stores rows in Postgres, but I have not decided whether I want long-lived integration coverage or a freeze-before-refactor workflow yet. Inspect the repo first and guide me toward the right testing path.
evals.expected_output: The agent inspects the repo, proposes a short scenario list, asks the user to choose integration, record-replay, or both, and keeps container orchestration on Testcontainers instead of assuming a broad matrix or a shell-driven harness.

evals.prompt: These Redis and Postgres tests are all mocked. I want live backend tests that stay useful after the first refactor, and I only need a few honest scenarios rather than every unit branch. Start from the current tests and recommend the right path.
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends the integration path, proposes a compact scenario shortlist after repo inspection, mines nearby unit tests for good payloads, and plans a Testcontainers-backed harness without drifting into record-replay unless the user chooses it.

evals.prompt: Freeze the current behavior of this Express endpoint before I port it to Hono. I want cassettes, explicit record and verify flows, and black-box checks that survive framework refactors. Inspect the repo and then let me choose the exact scenarios.
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends the record-replay path, proposes a small scenario menu after inspection, keeps the characterization harness source-level black-box, and describes cassette-based record and verify workflows on top of a Testcontainers-backed local topology.

evals.prompt: For this Azure Function, I want one ongoing integration happy path and also a freeze-before-refactor workflow for the risky branch. Inspect the repo, tell me what scenarios make sense, and then let me choose what to keep in integration, record-replay, or both.
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends the both path, proposes scenario classes after repo inspection, assigns them intentionally to integration, record-replay, or both, and builds one shared harness for the Functions host and Testcontainers-managed dependencies.

evals.prompt: Create backend tests for a worker that consumes broker messages, calls an outbound HTTP service, and writes to Blob Storage. I do not want a huge matrix. Inspect the codebase, suggest the best scenarios, and have me choose the workflow first.
evals.expected_output: The agent inspects the repo, suggests a small set of worker scenarios, asks the user to choose integration, record-replay, or both before implementation, and keeps the topology honest with a local worker boundary, local HTTP stub, and Testcontainers-managed dependencies.

evals.prompt: I want approval-style backend tests for a Node.js service that already has a Vitest live harness. Please keep the existing shared setup, add cassettes only where they belong, and let me choose the scenarios after you inspect the current suite.
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends the record-replay path, reuses the existing shared harness instead of cloning it, proposes scenario options after inspection, and layers cassette helpers and record/verify commands on top of the existing Testcontainers lifecycle.

evals.prompt: I need to decide between integration tests and behavior-freezing tests for a legacy Azure Function with Azurite and a Cosmos emulator. Inspect the repo first, propose the small set of scenarios worth paying for, and then let me pick the path.
evals.expected_output: The agent inspects the repo, recommends a path but still asks the user to choose integration, record-replay, or both, proposes a compact scenario list, and keeps Azurite and Cosmos orchestration on Testcontainers through the shared harness guidance.
